text:
Once upon a time in a tranquil village nestled among rolling hills, there lived a wise old owl named Olive. Olive never hooted during the day — she believed that everyone ought to sleep soundly. Instead, she filled her nights with stories. One evening, after a particularly stormy day, a young squirrel named Timothy scampered up to her branch.

"Why so quiet, Olive?" Timothy asked.

"I'm just dreaming," replied Olive. "Of a forest where a prince fell in love with a queen without a castle."

"Really?" Timothy was intrigued.

"Dreams are like that," Olive answered. "Sometimes we encounter enchanted characters."

Timothy perched beside her.

"Imagine then," started Olive, "a prince whose eyes glowed like beacons. Every time he smiled, flowers sprouted. But he had forgotten his duties."

"One prince, a forest full of love lights..."

"Yes!" Olive chuckled. "After many adventures, he remembered."

"And?" asked Timothy.
